texts.full_text: element in constant time. The operation that is O(logn) is insertion. As others have mentioned, pop is also O(logn) because it will remove the smallest (or largest) element, and then force a re-ordering to ensure thThe answer provided seems contradictory as you first state "O(\)" complexity but later mention that finding minimum/maximum in max or min heap takes logarithmic time. However if we are talking about just the operation of getting maximum (or mimimum) element, then it is O( \logn \*) for a binary-heap and not constant as mentioned by you earlier because to get this value from root node one has traverse down tree through multiple levels which takes time proportional with height. But if we talk about priority_queue (which uses heap internally) in C++, then it provides O( \ ) access for top element using its methods like 'top' and so on; but still insertion/dele
